python3 -c "
import csv
with open('/lustre/fast/fast/sgoel/forecasting/current_sim/final_runs_v37/opencode_aljazeeraQ12026v37_qwen36plus/26-05-02-00-01-06/agents/minimalHarness_qwen_qwen36-plus_001/workspace/market.csv') as f:
    reader = csv.DictReader(f)
    rows = list(reader)

for r in rows:
    rd = r.get('resolution_date','').strip()
    if rd and rd == '2026-02-18':
        print(f'QID: {r[\"qid\"]}')
        print(f'Title: {r[\"title\"]}')
        print(f'Background: {r[\"background\"][:300] if r[\"background\"] else \"N/A\"}')
        print('---')
"

QID: 207254
Title: Which U.S. government office will be identified as the source of the reposted comments that trigger the France-US diplomatic row by the end of February 2026?
Background: Question Start Date: 2026-02-17. U.S. embassies sometimes repost statements from Washington, and those posts can create diplomatic disputes if a foreign government views them as interference in domestic affairs.
---
QID: 485328
Title: Who will deliver the Democratic response to President Donald Trump’s State of the Union address in February 2026?
Background: Question Start Date: 2026-02-09. In the United States, the party opposing the president traditionally gives a televised response after the annual State of the Union address to Congress. Democratic leaders are expected to name one speaker for that role before the address.
---
QID: 943026
Title: What AI tool will a Dutch court bar from generating sexual imagery of real people without explicit permission by March 31, 2026?
Background: Question Start Date: 2026-02-17. A civil case in the Netherlands is examining whether an AI image-generation service can be held responsible when users create realistic sexual images of real people without consent. A ruling is expected later this month.
---
